mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized demand recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when the economy is in a post-shock recovery phase, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening labor demand and consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI inflation exceeds 0.4%, anticipated central bank tightening compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate robust household balance sheets and durable consumption demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal from HOUST and RSAFS yields negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion regime signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases.
